{
  "date": "2026-05-22",
  "snapshot_date": "2026-05-21",
  "logs_analysed": [
    "routine_2026-05-21_0600.log",
    "routine_2026-05-21_1000.log",
    "routine_2026-05-21_1400.log",
    "routine_2026-05-21_1800.log",
    "routine_2026-05-21_2300.log"
  ],
  "pipeline_health": {
    "runs_expected": 5,
    "runs_with_logs": 5,
    "errors_detected": 0,
    "notes": "All 5 scheduled runs present and completed cleanly (EXIT_CODE:0). Zero researcher crashes, zero WATCHDOG alerts, zero repair runs — a clean recovery after the May 20 researcher NameError crash (May 20 F004). Both Betfair and Matchbook scanners exited 0 in all 5 runs. Researcher and analyzer exited 0 in all 5 runs (15 markets, 60 credits each). The Betfair politics catalogue returned 2 markets for sport ID 2378961 in all 5 runs (the Irish Dáil by-elections: Dublin Central and Galway West, voting today 22 May 2026). Despite detecting these markets, the researcher correctly selected 0 politics/current-affairs in all 5 runs — consistent with the 3 open paper trades already placed on these markets on May 20 triggering the already-open dedup at the research-selection stage. Signals were generated in 3 of 5 runs (0 at 0600 and 1400; 2 each at 1000, 1800, and 2300) but zero new paper trades were logged: all signals were blocked by the high-odds lay cap (Top 6 Finish @ 4.20 odds, cap 4.0), the already-open dedup (Top 6 Finish and Top 4 Finish), or the Matchbook market-type edge cap (Top 4 Finish edge >= 25.0pp). Balance held at £271.25 (Reserved £75.59) throughout the day — the third consecutive day with zero new resolutions. The resolver checked 2 open-trade markets per run (SC Freiburg vs Aston Villa and one other); the SC Freiburg market remained stale in all 5 runs. 'WARNING: could not find injection point in HTML' appears in every dashboard deploy — cosmetic issue documented in prior audits, no functional impact. Distribution REVIEW DUE alert fires twice per run (7-9pp band, 33 resolved entries, threshold 12) — unactioned for day 7+. consec_losses=1 persisted throughout the day from the prior Match Odds loss."
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "platform",
      "title": "Betfair cross-ref index producing false confirmations: Brentford/Sunderland market-type mismatch shows 85-87pp phantom edge",
      "evidence": "The 1000 run analyzer log contains: 'BF cross-ref: Brentford MB=29.4% BF=97.1% claude=11.2% bf_edge=85.9pp confirms=True' and 'BF cross-ref: Sunderland MB=23.8% BF=96.2% claude=9.1% bf_edge=87.1pp confirms=True'. These same two cross-ref entries appeared in the May 20 2300 run and the May 20 2012 run (noted as a 'notable non-finding' in the May 20 audit narrative). The entries return confirms=True — meaning the cross-ref validator treats them as signal confirmations. No additional signals were generated from these entries (the Brentford and Sunderland signals at 11.2% and 9.1% Claude probability are both below the 12pp threshold). However,

    "notes": "All 5 scheduled runs present and completed cleanly (EXIT_CODE:0). Zero researcher crashes, zero WATCHDOG alerts, zero repair runs — fourth consecutive clean day. Both Betfair and Matchbook scanners exited 0 in all 5 runs. Researcher and analyzer exited 0 in all 5 runs (15 markets, 60 credits each run). Betfair politics catalogue (sport ID 2378961): returned 1 market in all 5 runs — the Makerfield by-election, first detected at the May 24 1400 run, continues to be detected today. Despite this, the researcher selected 0 politics/current-affairs in all 5 runs (platforms: ['matchbook']), consistent with the MOD-076 architecture where Betfair is cross-reference-only. Signal generation: 0 signals at 0600, 1000, 1400, and 1800; 3 signals at 2300 — all blocked by matchbook_no_backs (all 3 were back bets on Winner markets). Zero new paper trades logged across the entire day. One trade resolved between the 1800 and 2300 runs (open trades: 3→2), confirmed as a Matchbook back bet WIN (+£2.94, deduced from snapshot comparison: n=149→150, matchbook n=80→81, back n=40→41, PnL £182.75→£185.69). Balance remained at £282.75 (Reserved £8.50→£7.90, Available £274.25→£274.85) consistent with a single lay liability releasing at resolution. Notable infrastructure event: the 1000 run experienced a transient GitHub push failure for both smarkets-dashboard ('remote: Internal Server Error', Request ID BB38:1865A3:8EA92F:AADFB0:6A14103A, time 09:02:51Z) and betting-auditor ('remote: Internal Server Error', Request ID 9064:3DE64B:8E9CD0:AACBB9:6A14103C, time 09:02:54Z). The Pi handled this correctly: the betting-auditor commit had already been made locally (9beded6), a git fetch and rebase succeeded, and the push was retried at the 1400 run where it completed successfully (d3f400b→2ace1c2). No data was lost; the 1000 UTC log file is confirmed present in the repo. External search found no confirmed widespread GitHub outage on May 25, 2026, suggesting a transient infrastructure issue affecting specific repositories rather than a platform-wide event. The 2300 run's market selection was exclusively outright futures: all 15 markets were 'Winner' (0 Match Odds, 0 Moneyline, 0 Correct Score) — consistent with the late-night pattern where match-odds markets close after European fixtures end and only outright winner markets remain active. Dashboard 'WARNING: could not find injection point in HTML' appears in all 5 deploy runs — cosmetic, ongoing from prior days. Distribution REVIEW DUE alert fires twice per run (7-9pp band) in all 5 runs — unactioned for Day 12+. consec_losses=1 persisted throughout all 5 runs."

The analyzer found 2 signals (10pp+) at this run; only one reached the matchbook_no_backs check before the crash terminated the loop. Signal 2 — most likely a valid Matchbook lay or an allowed back on Match Odds/Moneyline — was never evaluated for paper trading and was silently dropped. Balance remained at £282.75 (unchanged from prior runs), consistent with no settlements or new trades being logged. EXIT_CODE:0 was reported in the run summary despite the crash — the paper_tracker runs as a subprocess and its non-zero exit code was not propagated to the top-level EXIT_CODE. The 1400 run also shows 'Distribution log updated: 3 new entries added' AFTER the crash, confirming the distr

      "evidence": "The 1400 run logged: 'Logged paper trade: Match Odds - BACK @ 87.72% (edge: 3.36pp) [betfair] | available now £240.43'. The snapshot recent_signals entry confirms edge_pct: 3.36, was_correct: true, paper_pnl: 0.66, resolved_at: 2026-05-28T22:03:52 (Madison Keys defeating Antonia Ruzic 6-4 6-4 in the French Open second round). The CLAUDE.md spec states the 10pp hard floor as an absolute block: 'Hard blocks: lays above 4.5 decimal odds (liability too asymmetric), edge below 10pp floor, effective stake below £0.50.' At 3.36pp this trade should have been blocked. The analyzer output for the 1400 run ('Found 4 signals (10pp+)') also does not account for a sub-10pp signal — the 3.36pp back was either passed through by a separate code path or not counted by the analyzer's threshold filter. The distribution_logger correctly classified the trade as 'near_certainty' (6 total / 4 resolved after logging, later 5 resolved after settlement), consistent with a probability-based class

Matchbook n=81, 50.6% WR, -£15.12 PnL — frozen for three consecutive full days. Signal counts (10pp+): 10 (0600), 9 (1000), 4 (1400), 2 (1800), 2 (2300). New paper trades: 7 (0600), 0 (1000), 1 (1400), 1 (1800), 0 (2300) = 9 total, all Betfair. Three settlements: two Match Odds losses resolved between 1000 and 1400 runs (balance drop £376.59→£368.99, -£7.60; De Minaur LAY -£2.60 and one Match Odds BACK -£5.00, both at ~11:00 UTC per resolved_at timestamps); one Match Odds BACK loss resolved at 2300 run (8.56pp sub-floor trade, -£5.00). Total resolved on May 29: +3, all losses, -£12.60 combined. Running PnL: £276.59 → £263.99. Balance at end of day: £363.99 reserved £176.00 available £187.99. Consecutive loss counter: consec_losses=1 at 2300 run after De Minaur BACK loss. Betfair cross-ref index sizes: 4888 (0600), 4502 (1000), 4770 (1400), 4546 (1800), 4127 (2300).
